titleStmt.title: Memorandum by Edmund A. Gullion to the Director of the Policy Planning Staff ( (Bowie))

div.head:
Memorandum by Edmund A. Gullion
Member of the Policy Planning Staff. to the Director of the Policy Planning Staff ( Bowie )

list.head: Subject:
list.item: Further French Defeat in VietnamTelegram 2928 from Saigon, June 29, reported the destruction in central Vietnam of Groupe Mobile 100, consisting mainly of the two French battalions which had served in Korea. (751G.00/6–2954)

div.p: At Ankhe, far down the coast of Annam, the French have just suffered their third greatest defeat in Indochina—after Dien Bien Phu, and Cao Bong-Langson (1950/51).
They have lost over a thousand men, including their two Korea battalions, over a hundred trucks, some guns and munitions. This is by far the biggest reverse they have ever known in the South.

It does not augur well for building a strong South Vietnam. It
shows Viet Minh regulars in strength and an irregular strength greater than supposed and still growing.

It may enable the Viet Minh to demand an enclave in the South, here or at Hui, or Can Tho, in exchange for Haiphong. This can only mean that the Communists will eventually dominate in the South as in the North.
